title: hieracium bifidum subsp. expallidulum
intro: Hieracium bifidum subsp. expallidulum, also known as a lesser hawk's beard, is a rare, flowering plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It possesses medicinal properties due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation and gastrointestinal disorders. The health benefits of Hieracium bifidum subsp. expallidulum include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve digestion, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by drying and infusing the leaves, flowers, or roots in hot water or oil, and can be used as a tea, tincture, or topical application.
